definition: The eight squares around a Standing player. An enemy standing in one is [[marked]].
Standing in an opponent's [[tackle-zone]] — what forces a dodge to leave and adds −1 to many actions.
Moving beyond your MA, one square at a time; each rush needs a 2+ (a natural 1 falls). Up to two per move.
Ends your team turn at once — a fumbled pick-up or pass, going down with the ball, or the ball reaching the opposition.
Knocked down but face-up. Can't act; standing up costs 3 squares of movement. Exerts no [[tackle-zone]].
Face-down and unable to be activated. Turned face-up to [[prone]] at the end of your turn.
Can't catch, intercept or give [[assist|assists]]; recovers at the end of your turn.
A Block result that knocks both players down unless they have [[block]] or [[wrestle]].
A Block result that shoves the target one square away; the blocker may [[follow-up]].
A Block result that pushes the target back and knocks them down.
Put [[prone]] (or [[stunned]]) — which triggers an Armour roll.
Moving for free into the square a pushed-back player has vacated.
A nearby team-mate adding ±1 Strength to a block — offensive (yours) or defensive (theirs).
A Block combined with movement; one player may Blitz per team turn.
Attacking a [[prone]] or [[stunned]] opponent; one per turn, and the fouler risks being sent off.
A serious-injury result — roll on the casualty table for the outcome.
